title: Suki's Great Cake Mix-Up
synopsis: When Suki accidentally mixes up her ingredients while baking a birthday cake for her friend Maya, she learns that mistakes can lead to wonderful surprises and that friends love you no matter what.

pages.text: Suki bounced out of bed. Today was special! It was her best friend Maya's birthday. Suki had a wonderful plan. She would bake Maya the most amazing cake ever. 'This will be the best birthday surprise,' Suki said to herself. She put on her favorite purple apron with dancing cupcakes on it.

illustration.description: A cheerful Asian girl with pigtails bouncing out of bed, wearing pajamas with star patterns. Her bedroom has bright yellow walls, colorful toys scattered around, and morning sunlight streaming through the window. She has big expressive eyes and a huge smile.
illustration.alt: Suki jumping out of bed excitedly on a sunny morning

pages.text: Suki ran to the kitchen. Her mom was drinking coffee and reading. 'Good morning, sunshine,' Mom said. 'I'm making Maya's birthday cake today!' Suki announced. 'That's wonderful, honey. Do you need help?' Mom asked. 'Nope! I can do it all by myself,' Suki said proudly.

illustration.description: Suki in her purple cupcake apron standing in a bright kitchen next to her mom, who has curly brown hair and kind eyes. The kitchen has turquoise cabinets, a yellow countertop, and morning light. Mom is holding a steaming coffee mug.
illustration.alt: Suki talking to her mom in the colorful kitchen

pages.text: Suki gathered all her ingredients on the counter. She needed flour, sugar, eggs, and butter. 'Let's see,' she said, counting on her fingers. 'Flour, sugar, eggs, butter. Check, check, check, and check!' She felt very grown-up doing everything herself. This cake would be perfect for Maya.

illustration.description: Suki standing on a small stool at the kitchen counter, surrounded by baking ingredients. Bags of flour and sugar, a carton of eggs, and butter are neatly arranged. She's counting on her fingers with a determined expression and big bright eyes.
illustration.alt: Suki organizing baking ingredients on the kitchen counter

pages.text: Suki started mixing. First, she added the flour. Or was it sugar? The bags looked very similar. 'This looks right,' she said, dumping the white powder into her bowl. Then she cracked the eggs. SPLISH! Some eggshell fell in too. 'Oops!' Suki giggled.

illustration.description: Suki with flour dusting her cheeks and apron, holding a cracked eggshell over a mixing bowl. Her expression is surprised but amused. The kitchen counter is messy with spilled flour, and there are eggshell pieces visible in the bowl.
illustration.alt: Suki making a mess while cracking eggs for the cake

pages.text: Next came the butter. But Suki grabbed the salt instead! She added three big spoonfuls. 'This seems like a lot,' she thought. But she kept going. Then she added what she thought was sugar. It was actually flour again! 'Perfect!' Suki said, not noticing her mistakes.

illustration.description: Suki pouring salt from a container labeled 'SALT' into her mixing bowl, but she's looking away distractedly. The salt container is clearly visible to the viewer. Her expression is happy and confident, unaware of her mistake.
illustration.alt: Suki accidentally adding salt instead of sugar to her cake batter

pages.text: Suki mixed and mixed. The batter looked very strange. It was lumpy and gray. 'Maybe all cake batter looks like this,' she wondered. She poured it into a pan. Into the oven it went! 'Maya will love this!' Suki said happily. She couldn't wait to surprise her friend.

illustration.description: Suki holding a cake pan with gray, lumpy batter that looks quite unappetizing. She has a hopeful, excited expression with sparkles in her big eyes. The oven door is open behind her, glowing warm orange.
illustration.alt: Suki putting her strange-looking cake batter into the oven

pages.text: DING! The timer went off. Suki opened the oven. Her cake was... very flat. And very hard. And it smelled funny. 'Oh no!' Suki cried. 'This doesn't look like a birthday cake at all!' She poked it with a fork. It was hard as a rock!

illustration.description: Suki looking shocked and dismayed as she peers into the oven at a completely flat, dark, burnt-looking cake. Her eyes are wide with surprise and her mouth is open in an 'oh no' expression. Steam and odd-colored smoke waft from the failed cake.
illustration.alt: Suki discovering her cake has turned out terribly wrong

pages.text: Just then, the doorbell rang. DING DONG! 'That's Maya!' Suki gasped. What would she do now? She couldn't give Maya this terrible cake! Suki quickly hid the pan behind her back. 'Hi Maya!' she said nervously when she opened the door.

illustration.description: Suki at the front door greeting Maya, a cheerful Black girl with bouncy curls and a bright yellow dress. Suki is hiding the cake pan behind her back with a nervous smile. Maya looks excited and happy, carrying a small gift bag.
illustration.alt: Suki nervously greeting Maya at the door while hiding the failed cake

pages.text: 'I made you a birthday cake,' Suki said sadly. 'But it didn't turn out right.' She showed Maya the flat, hard cake. Maya looked at it and started to laugh! 'It looks like a pancake rock!' Maya giggled. 'A pancake rock?' Suki asked, starting to smile too.

illustration.description: Both girls looking at the failed cake together, with Maya laughing joyfully and Suki beginning to smile. Maya is pointing at the cake with delight. The flat, dark cake does indeed look like a weird rock. Both girls have bright, expressive eyes full of mirth.
illustration.alt: Maya and Suki laughing together at the cake that looks like a rock

pages.text: 'You know what?' Maya said. 'This is the funniest cake ever! No one has ever made me a pancake rock before!' Maya hugged Suki tight. 'But you can't eat it,' Suki said, still worried. 'So what?' Maya laughed. 'We can use it as a doorstop! Or a paperweight!'

illustration.description: Maya giving Suki a big warm hug in the kitchen, both girls smiling widely. The failed cake sits on the counter nearby. Maya's expression is full of joy and acceptance, while Suki looks relieved and happy. The kitchen is filled with warm, golden light.
illustration.alt: Maya hugging Suki and making her feel better about the cake mistake

pages.text: Suki's mom came into the kitchen. She saw the girls laughing and the strange cake. 'What happened here?' she asked, trying not to smile. 'Suki made me a pancake rock for my birthday!' Maya announced proudly. 'It's the most creative cake ever!' Mom looked at the cake and chuckled. 'How about we make cookies together instead?' she suggested.

illustration.description: All three characters in the kitchen - Suki's mom with her hands on her hips looking amused, Maya still giggling and pointing at the cake, and Suki looking much happier now. The kitchen scene is warm and loving, with the infamous flat cake prominently displayed.
illustration.alt: Suki's mom discovering the cake situation with both girls laughing

pages.text: The three of them made delicious chocolate chip cookies together. Maya said it was the best birthday ever. 'I learned something today,' Suki said. 'Even when things go wrong, they can still be fun with a good friend.' Maya grinned. 'And I learned that pancake rocks make the best paperweights!' They all laughed together.

illustration.description: All three characters around the kitchen table with a plate of golden chocolate chip cookies, everyone smiling and laughing. The failed cake sits in the background like a funny reminder. The scene is warm and cozy, filled with the golden light of late afternoon and the joy of friendship and family.
illustration.alt: Suki, Maya, and mom enjoying fresh cookies together at the kitchen table

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was Suki making for Maya's birthday?
questions.explanation: Suki wanted to make Maya a birthday cake as a special surprise for her best friend.

questions.question: What did Suki accidentally add instead of sugar?
questions.explanation: Suki mixed up several ingredients - she added salt instead of sugar and flour instead of sugar at different times.

questions.question: How did Maya react when she saw the failed cake?
questions.explanation: Maya laughed when she saw the flat, hard cake and said it looked like a 'pancake rock,' which made Suki feel better.

questions.question: What did they make together at the end?
questions.explanation: Suki's mom suggested they make chocolate chip cookies together, which turned out deliciously.

questions.question: What did Suki learn from this experience?
choices: Things can still be fun with a good friend even when they go wrong
questions.explanation: Suki learned that even when things don't go as planned, they can still be enjoyable when you have a good friend who cares about you.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Help complete the story by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Suki wanted to make Maya a birthday ___1___. She mixed up the ingredients and added ___2___ instead of sugar. When Maya saw the flat cake, she ___3___ and called it a pancake rock!

pages.title: What Happened Next?
pages.instructions: Choose the words that tell what happened in the story!
pages.text: Maya ___1___ Suki a big hug. Then Suki's ___2___ helped them make delicious chocolate chip ___3___. Maya said it was the ___4___ birthday ever!
